transcript.clean_text: hello and welcome to highlights of state seven of the Tour de France on GCN racing before we get on to today's stage let's quickly recap yesterday because it was fantastic on a mountainous day but one of relentless climbing Dillon Tunes was the strongest of two survivors from a big breakaway group in the group of overall contenders not far behind yellow jersey ala philippe was looking ominously strong as was Garant thomas best of the group of GC favorites but Anna Philippe's attack at the end was not enough to save his yellow jersey which goes to Julia Ciccone second on the stage but that is some consolation prize on to today and stage 7 offered some respite from the climbing it was mostly flats between Belfort and shallow sauce song but at 230 k's it's the longest stage of the entire race and at 27 degrees at sunny you can't say it's a bad day to ride a bike for six hours but the wind would be in the riders faces all day slowing the pace and making it even longer in fact it's a brave rider that would want to forge a breakaway on a day like that but to dutifully attacked from the gun both no stranger to breakaways in this year's tour Stefan receta of confidence and yoanna Fredo of 1c group do bear the bunch as you can see were only too happy to let them go but as ever with a race like the Tour though even on a day like today with its gentle roads and relaxed early pace you can't switch off for a moment a crash at the back of the peloton was a rude reminder of that fact and worst off was TJ van garderen Aikido mystique for the EF team now covered in road rash as you can see his morale will no doubt be even worse than it was yesterday when he was dropped surprisingly prematurely on the climb to the finish and to make matters worse there was still 200 kilometers to go the gap to the break up to 4 minutes and 23 the mood in the bunch was seemingly pretty relaxed here we got check only the yellow jersey we're catching up with fellow Italians matteo trenton him inch Enzoani bleah in fact they're probably so relaxed because they knew that Tony Martin of yumbo bhisma was setting the pace on the front of the bunch something he's done for so many years he could probably do it in his sleep and he also had fame support from the quranic' quickstep and sued a lotto so there seemed little doubt for the outcome of that duo out front now nothing of note really happened at all until 33 case to go and that intermediate sprint receta rolled over first in the breakaway but in contrast back in the bunch team Bahrain Morita while lighting it up for their man sonic or Bradley who Julie edged out the green jersey where a Peter Sagan and Elia Viviani then just as we saw on day one this surprise lifting the pace caught a few riders out Wow fan art a key member of the yumbo bhisma lead-out train for one but perhaps more notably overall contenders nairo quintana and Dan Martin today they were forgiven for their negligence and they regained contact with the peloton after a few kilometres but it's not a great sign for Martin that this is the second time it's happened in just a week for him the break was finally brought back but not before Johanna Fredo put in a symbolic attack to eke out a few more meters in front enough to ensure that he earned the prize of the day's most combative rider with 12 days to go it was all together then the pace was high seemingly every team trying to stay up front be they teams with sprinters eyeing up the stage win or overall contenders looking to stay out of trouble as a result the pace of the bunch rocketed up to 50 K is an hour and then with just a few K to go eunbok bhisma would clearly be glad that Webber not regain contact as he pulled for two kilometers at 65 K's an hour in his first Tour de France this is confirmation that the three-time cyclocross world champion is freakishly talented on the road as well now into the final kilometer and to connect quickstep took over pace setting duties Michael Moore Cove leading a bunch through that final turn and looking incredibly smooth given the power he's putting out to connect quick set raw looking very good in frack max 4kz the final lead out man for Viviani ready to launch his man who just over 200 meters to go but coming up on the right-hand side of your picture was Dillon grown a vague and looking like he had an almighty turn of speed again - kicking hard and Caleb Ewan storms pass to foundering Viviani from that angle it looks like a blanket finish grown of Aegon claims it but the aerial shot here from the helicopter shows that it a hair's breadth that separates you and and grown of Aegon and I don't think it was until that photo finish that we got the confirmation the grown of Aegon was looking for his first stage win in this year's Tour de France the third stage win for his young Bo bhisma team there we go then at the confirmation of the results confirmation that no one sprinter seems dominant this year in the tour in fact we've had a different winner every day at the race so far groan of Aegon though has got to be happy with that after missing out on the Sprint on stage one which was taken by his teammate he'll be half of a monkey off his back after that not much action in the general classification today Ciccone gets another day in yellow tomorrow ala Philippe just six seconds back there and all the main GC riders waiting in the wings tomorrow stage does not look like one for the sprinters in fact it may well be one for the break again 200 kilometers from mccourt society n5 second cat climbs as you can see and 2/3 cats so it's going to be a tough day for all the riders remember that you can watch the action on Eurosport if you live in Europe and you can of course as I
